posts.caption:
It’s not summer, it’s WAG season. The original Baden-Baden WAG, #VictoriaBeckham, has just offered a glimpse of what football wife style looks like 20 years on from her iconic appearances at the 2006 World Cup USA vs Paraguay match.
Gone are the oversized sunglasses, the even bigger Hermès Birkin and the nano shorts. Posh Spice has grown up and so has her wardrobe. In place of hot pants are her signature pleated trousers, presumably courtesy of her own label, although the pinstriped waistcoat feels like it might just be a subtle love note her Baden-Baden era
Some things, it seems, never change. Tap the link in bio to read the full story.

posts.caption:
Today Keir Starmer has announced a full social media ban for under 16s in the UK. This will follow the Australian model and ‘will include platforms like Snapchat, TikTok, YouTube, Instagram, Facebook and X,’ according to a Government statement. Messaging services like WhatsApp and Signal are not expected to be affected.
The legislation is set to go through around Christmas, with a ban coming into force early next year. While the announcement has been met with praise from many campaigners and the opposition, some critics believe it is a blunt instrument that doesn’t do enough to tackle the type of content social media companies allow to be posted online.
Do you welcome the ban? Let us know below.

posts.caption:
Fashion has no shortage of beautiful faces, but @alexconsani represents a new generation of supermodels—the ones not just walking the runway but creating platforms, building communities, and leading with personality. First gaining attention on TikTok, Consani is now one of the world’s most sought after models. “I’m having fun,” says Consani. “I’m doing it for all 50 of the other bitches that would love to be in that room.” For Bazaar’s Summer 2026 issue, the supermodel talks about trying her hand at acting, moving into her big-girl home, and entering her “experimental era.”
-

posts.caption:
The first official portrait of Barack Obama and Michelle Obama together was revealed yesterday in a private gathering at the Obama Presidential Center in Chicago. The painting, by Nigeria-born, Los Angeles-based artist Njideka Akunyili Crosby, is one of a multitude of landmark art commissions at the center, which will be inaugurated with a star-studded gala later this week and opens to the public on Juneteenth. For our Summer issue, we visited Akunyili Crosby at her L.A. studio as she was in the process of completing this historic work and spoke with her about the instincts and ideas behind it. How does one approach painting a couple that is among the most photographed in the world, if not history? “It’s something I’m still thinking about every day,” she says. At the link in our bio, she opens up to Bazaar about why capturing what the former president and first lady represent feels so important right now.  “Tradition is not untouchable,” she says. “We can own it, we can shape it, we can move it the way we need.”
-
Photography: @joshuawoods and @obamafoundation
Story: @laurenmichelejackson
Artwork: @njidekaakunyilicrosby

posts.caption:
Prince Harry and Meghan Markle have released a statement in response to Prime Minister Sir Keir Starmer’s announcement that under-16s will be banned from social media platforms including TikTok, Snapchat and Instagram.
The couple have embraced the announcement as a ‘welcome step forward’, saying that there needs to be a commitment to putting children’s wellbeing ahead of engagement and profit.
They stated that the burden can’t solely rest on parents and children, but it also must be borne by the companies as well.

posts.caption:
As the World Cup spotlight turns toward New York, the city is getting its own jersey to match. ⁠
⁠
Spearheaded by NYC Mayor Zohran Mamdani in collaboration with @arshraziuddin and @mazzi_sports, the project celebrates the city's role on soccer's biggest stage while embracing the diversity that defines New York itself. ⁠
⁠
Rather than creating another exclusive collector's piece, the team took a more democratic approach: only 1,500 jerseys will be produced, but each is priced at just $50, making it accessible to the fans and communities that make the city what it is. They're currently available, only in person, at @citystorenyc.

posts.caption:
Some archive pieces stay buried. Others come back with a point to prove. For its latest collaboration with @beams_mens_casual, @smithoptics has reopened the vault and revived the Sliders, a silhouette first seen in the late ’90s.⁠
⁠
Dressed in an exclusive AB3 colorway, the frame pairs a black finish with orange accents, a subtle nod to BEAMS' identity while preserving the model's distinct attitude.⁠
⁠
The nostalgia ends where the performance begins. Updated with SMITH's interchangeable lens system, the sunglasses arrive fitted with a custom ChromaPop photochromic lens that adapts to changing light. An Asian-fit nose pad ensures all-day comfort, while a bespoke collaborative case completes the package.⁠
⁠
The SMITH x BEAMS Slider sunglasses are currently available for US$227 via SMITH's online shop.

posts.caption:
For a brand as influential as @denimtears , a dedicated bag line feels long overdue. Following its collaboration with @marcjacobs on a signature tote, @tremaineemory is finally expanding the label’s universe with its first standalone collection of bags.
There were many things Emory had in mind when designing the collection, chief among them functionality. “Unless you’re extremely wealthy, most people can’t afford a different bag for every outfit. So you want the few bags you have to work across different looks,” Emory says.
Arriving for Spring/Summer 2026, the range features Denim Tears’ signature chain-stitch embroidery across cotton canvas and leather. The campaign was photographed by Gabriel Moses, and the collection is now available.

posts.caption:
@labseries & @jordanclarksons are over looksmaxxing, and you should be too.
With the culture around optimizing wellness finally losing steam, the New York-born skincare brand is betting that more men are looking for something grounded in reality. As conversations around grooming continue to evolve, Lab Series has spent decades developing products specifically designed for men’s skin.
Now, with the Knicks guard subbing in to champion the brand’s refreshed perspective, Lab Series and Clarkson are looking to “Turn Your Routine Pro,” prioritizing products backed by expertise and routines built around real life over quick fixes and viral hacks.
Click the link in bio for more.

posts.caption:
There are people who wear time, and then there are people who define it. @palaktiwarii belongs to the second kind.✨
She carries something rare, a weight that isn’t borrowed from the past, but isn’t chasing the future either. It simply exists, with the kind of quiet confidence that doesn’t need to announce itself.
In this frame, legacy and the now collapse into each other. @guesswatches on her wrist, intent in her eyes, highlighting the “Main Character” hunger behind the gold hardware; this is a story of a woman who isn’t just wearing the accessories of success, but is actively forging them. ⌚️
The Architecture of Time isn’t a concept. It’s a posture. A way of owning every second without letting a single one own you.
@palaktiwarii doesn’t follow the tempo. She sets it. And right now, she’s setting it for all of us. 🫶🏻

posts.caption:
You can’t talk about Danish design without referencing HAY. ⁠
⁠
Since the brand was founded almost 25 years ago, it’s not only become wildly successful, but it’s also shifted the perceptions of what “good” design should look like, and – crucially – who can afford it.⁠
⁠
The brainchild of husband-and-wife duo Rolf and Mette Hay, the eponymous brand began in Copenhagen, where its headquarters remain. ⁠
⁠
Ahead of the city being taken over by @3daysofdesign, we caught up with the Hays (although trying to pin them down requires almost as much coordination as launching a collection, and when we finally connect, they’re in different countries).⁠
⁠
Head to the link in bio to read the full feature. ⁠
⁠
Photography: courtesy of HAY

posts.alt_text:
Maintaining a low profile without losing your audience is a rare trick for a musician. It requires a certain panache and careful execution. For many artists, say Frank Ocean, Salem, or pre-2021 Playboi Carti, sporadic Instagram stories and rare public appearances have become tools for crafting a “mysterious,” almost untouchable persona. But for Houston-based multihyphenate reggie (@reginaldhelmsjr), that’s nowhere near the reason he went quiet. 

Since dropping his first solo song in 2020, the artist has uploaded 11 videos to YouTube, nine solo songs to streaming platforms, and hasn’t posted on Instagram or X since 2022. Despite the radio silence, his audience never left, anxiously awaiting the next chapter of his Southern-dripping soulful sonic storytelling.

“It wasn’t me trying to be mysterious or purposely hide from the spotlight,” reggie says about his years-long hiatus, which he initiated promptly after hitting the peak of his career. But, by any means, his hop off the grid was not a move that was purposely pencilled into his timeline or a decision he lost sleep over. “It’s how I talk too. I don’t really like to talk unless what I’m saying is super needed in that specific moment. I don’t just yap to yap.”

That intentionality runs through everything — his silences, his output, and ultimately ‘UNDRA’, his debut album arriving nearly five years after his first solo release and long overdue by any conventional measure.

Five years, nine singles, and one debut album that almost never came; reggie is finally ready to formally introduce himself.

Full interview at the link in bio. 

Words by Elaina Bernstein @elainalaurenn 
Photos by Spencer Stovell @spyonspeezy 
Styling by Le Pere @lepere / Manni Arango @handymanni 
Production by Sarah Schecker @sarahschecker 
Grooming by Seiya Iibuchi @seiya_makeup

posts.caption:
@jennifer.garner *always* knows what to do (and it usually involves sorghum).
Between starring in summer’s buzziest new series, taking her near-billion dollar company public, and turning ‘Pretend Cooking Show’ into a reality, she still manages to be the nicest woman in Hollywood.
Playing a widowed food blogger in @Peacock’s adaptation of @elinhilderbrand’s bestseller ‘The Five-Star Weekend,’ the beloved actress and entrepreneur channels her inner Ina Garten for a not-so-Instagrammable girls’ weekend on Nantucket with co-stars-turned-IRL besties, @chloesevigny, @morereginahall, @gemmachan and @darcycarden, exploring what it means to grow and develop in middle age.
At 54, the Marvel Hero and supermom reflects on her genre-spanning career, being “total besties” with John Cena, her surprisingly vast knowledge of weaponry, plus the responsibility of celebrities to stand up for working locally for InStyle’s Summer Imagemakers Issue at the link in @instylemagazine’s bio now.

So naturally, we thought of some of our favorite iconic besties (some IRL and some fictional) that we believe embody the love #OliviaRodrigo captures on the track.

Swipe through to see our faves, and feel free to tag your own bestie down below.

#OutandProud: Few designers have built a world quite like Ayushman “Bobo” Mitra (@bobocalcutta). As the founder of Bobo Calcutta, he has spent years creating a universe where art, fashion, memory, and storytelling collide, resulting in one of Indian fashion’s most distinctive creative voices.
In conversation with LSA, Mitra reflects on the future of Bobo Calcutta, the enduring value of craftsmanship, queer visibility in fashion, and the people, places, and passions that continue to shape his work.
Read the full story at the link in bio.
[LSA Out and Proud, Ayushman Mitra, Bobo Calcutta, Pride, Pride Month]
posts.alt_text:
It’s official, Foo Fighters (@foofighters) are making their India debut in 2027. The Rock ‘n’ Roll Hall of Famers are finally joining a long list of rock veterans to bring their talents to the country as part of their ongoing ‘Take Cover’ tour. 

It’s exciting because this will be fresh off the release of their 12th Studio album, ‘Your Favorite Toy,’ a record made by a band that seems  reinvigorated with new drummer Ilan Rubin bringing a newfound energy to their sound.
 
They will be joined in both Mumbai and Bangalore by New York rock act The Pretty Reckless and Austin-based act Die Spitz along with local rock acts Pinkshift , Alain Johannes , Still In Therapy and Mali. 

Tickets for the Mumbai and Bengaluru concerts will go on sale on June 16, 2026 on BookMyShow.

Head to the link in bio for more details.

posts.caption:
Yes, it’s L’OFFICIEL! Cosa hanno in comune “Dune: Part Two”, “Furiosa: A Mad Max Saga” e la nuova serie Apple TV “Lucky”? Sono tutti ambientati nel deserto—e tutti vedono protagonista @AnyaTaylorJoy. «Mi diverte sempre, perché non capisco cosa ci sia nei miei capelli, nel mio incarnato o nella larghezza dei miei occhi che mi renda adatta a interpretare una creatura del deserto. Eppure la gente adora mettermi lì», confessa Taylor-Joy a L’OFFICIEL. «Avverto una vera affinità con questi mondi».
Dal pianeta brullo di Arrakis in “Dune” al Wasteland che domina “Furiosa: A Mad Max Saga”, la nostra protagonista di giugno continua a ritrovarsi negli scenari più ostili del cinema contemporaneo. «Il deserto possiede una sacralità propria», osserva. Alla domanda su cosa ami di questi spazi desolati, la risposta è immediata: «L’alba e il tramonto, soprattutto. Perché vedi i colori più straordinari e senti di essere l’unica persona sulla terra».
Global Chief Content Officier @giampietrobaudo
Photography and Art Direction @szilvesztermako
Styled by @lisajarvis_stylist
Writer @swannsees
Casting @laurentabach
Hair @gregoryrussellhair
Makeup @georgieisdellmakeup using @diorbeauty
Manicure @kimkimnails

posts.caption:
Introducing our global cover star, @anyataylorjoy.
“I’ve always been nomadic by nature,” the British-Argentine actor tells L’OFFICIEL. “Now I’ve just fully embraced it as part of who I am. If I have a couple of days off, I’m always like, ‘Ooh, what’s within driving distance?’ If it’s six hours or less, I’m like, ‘Let’s go. Why not?’”
Fresh off of a recent desert road trip, Taylor-Joy—who has cemented her status as a fashion darling through ambassadorships with @Dior and @TiffanyandCo—is preparing for the release of Apple TV’s ‘Lucky,’ a thriller about a con artist caught in the fallout of a multimillion-dollar heist gone wrong. The role marks another chapter in a career spanning ‘The Witch,’ ‘The Queen’s Gambit,’ ‘Furiosa,’ and ‘Dune.’
In ‘Lucky,’ her character is pursued by the FBI and a ruthless mob boss while searching for something more elusive than freedom: stability. “I was so attracted to playing somebody who was just so desperate to stay still and put down roots,” says Taylor-Joy.
Filmed across the desert and inside Las Vegas’s Caesars Palace, the production left its mark. “I have a real soft spot for Caesars Palace now—we’ve been through a lot together,” she laughs.
At the link in bio, read our full conversation with Taylor-Joy on inhabiting new characters, life on the road, and ‘Lucky.’

Direzione Rio de Janeiro. @LudovicDeSaintSernin rilancia il suo universo swimwear donna con tre nuove silhouette — Bikini, Trikini e Bodysuit, in nero, bianco e marrone — e per celebrarlo sceglie la città che più di ogni altra ha fatto del corpo un linguaggio. La campagna SS26, fotografata da Jorge Perez Ortiz e con styling di Pau Avia, si muove tra l’architettura modernista di una residenza firmata Oscar Niemeyer a São Conrado e le spiagge battute dal sole, le rocce costiere e l’Atlantico aperto. Volto della campagna la modella brasiliana Barbara Valente con un cast interamente brasiliano. Il swimwear come estensione dell’identità — libertà, sensualità, espressione di sé.
Photo: Jorge Perez Ortiz
#LudovicDeSaintSernin #LOfficiel

posts.caption:
Tutto ebbe inizio nel 1922, con la scoperta della tomba di Tutankhamon. Da quel momento, @VanCleefArpels non ha mai smesso di guardare all’Antico Egitto come fonte d’ispirazione. Tra il 1923 e il 1925, la maison creò una serie di pezzi che traducevano i simboli egizi nel linguaggio dell’Art Déco — geometrie, contrasti netti, offerte, geroglifici, sfingi, fiori di loto. Smeraldi, zaffiri, rubini buff-top e onice per rendere motivi delicati e preziosi. Creazioni rarissime, oggi ambitissime dai collezionisti — e radice storica della nuova collezione Fascinating Egypt. La storia non si conserva dietro un vetro. Si rilegge.
Photo: Courtesy of @VanCleefArpels
#VanCleefArpels #HighJewellery #LOfficiel #LOfficielItalia

posts.caption:
Welcome to the June issue, where L’OFFICIEL explores travel not as movement, but as a state of mind. Our “nomadic by nature” cover star @AnyaTaylorJoy discusses her love of road trips, filming the new Apple TV series ‘Lucky’ in the Las Vegas desert, and her unique approach to learning her characters. The enigmatic photographer @SzilveszterMako, renowned for his surreal images, cinematic backdrops, and dreamlike scenes, turned his lens on the actress, capturing her shifting world for our shoot.
Elsewhere, the issue focuses on slow travel and the art of pausing. We take you to Grand Cayman, Ischia, and Costa Rica, where hydrotherapy and relaxation define the experience, and across the Canadian Rockies, Arabian Desert, and Australian Outback by rail—the defining way to travel in 2026. Elsewhere, we follow one writer into the next frontier of luxury wellness: sleep optimization.
In Paris, we sit down with Hong Kong–born couturier @RobertWun, who has made a name for himself with dramatic, over-the-top silhouettes; he reminds us that slowing down is sometimes the most radical imaginative act. And in Tbilisi, it’s the creative class who are putting the Georgian capital on the map—and we have your guide to everything that’s happening now.
On the fashion front, Spring/Summer 2026’s finest looks head to Cape Town for a sun-drenched escape, while long-heralded summer heroes—unexpected denim and easy dresses—capture the spirit of going nowhere in good company, this time in Los Angeles’s canyons. Stay tuned for our issue to hit newsstands this month.

posts.caption:
L'OFFICIEL's latest travel obsession isn't a destination—it's how you get there.⁠
⁠
As travelers increasingly seek experiences that prioritize presence over speed, train travel is enjoying a renaissance. Once a symbol of old-world glamour immortalized in films like 'Murder on the Orient Express' and 'The Darjeeling Limited,' rail journeys are once again capturing the imagination of modern travelers.⁠
⁠
From the iconic Venice Simplon-Orient-Express to scenic routes through the Canadian Rockies, the Scottish Highlands, and beyond, today's luxury trains offer an alternative to the rush of contemporary travel. With panoramic views, refined interiors, and a pace that encourages travelers to savor every mile, the journey itself becomes part of the destination. In an era defined by constant connectivity and packed itineraries, train travel offers something increasingly rare: the chance to slow down and enjoy the ride.⁠ ⁠
⁠
At the link in our bio, discover the most spectacular train journeys to book now.⁠
⁠
Writer @fifival⁠
Photos courtesy of ⁠La Dolce Vita Orient Express, Belmond, Rocky Mountaineer ⁠
⁠
#summertravel #traintravel2026 #summer2026 #vacationdestinations⁠

posts.caption:
L'OFFICIEL's summer travel itinerary is packed with must-visit destinations—but not at the expense of a good night's sleep. In fact, we've carved out a dedicated stop for the latest wellness obsession: "sleep-maxxing."⁠
⁠
The newest frontier in luxury wellness isn’t another cold plunge, NAD drip, or biohacking trend — it’s sleep optimization. As “sleepmaxxing” takes over social media, wellness destinations like SHA Mexico are reframing sleep as the foundation of longevity, recovery, and overall health. From nervous system evaluations to sleep-focused rituals, the message is clear: no amount of wellness trends can compensate for poor rest. In a culture obsessed with doing more, the real reset may come from something much simpler — consistent sleep, quiet routines, and learning how to truly slow down.⁠ At the link in our bio, take the plunge in pursuit of a good night’s rest. ⁠
⁠
Writer @⁠fifival⁠
⁠
Photos courtesy of Ananda, SHA Mexico, Carillon Miami Wellness Resort

posts.caption:
Go New York, Go New York, Go! The @nyknicks won their first NBA championship since 1973, ending a 53-year wait for New York fans. They defeated the San Antonio Spurs in Game 5 last night to bring the Larry O’Brien Trophy back to New York. And unlike in ‘How to Lose a Guy in 10 Days,’ nobody was asking for a soda during this Knicks game. Congrats to the Knicks!
#nyknicks #nbafinals #knicks

posts.caption:
@Balenciaga dropped its Spring Summer 2026 preview collection in Mumbai last night, and the energy in the room felt different.
With Fatima Sana Sheikh, Bobby Deol, Mouni Roy, Sanya Malhotra, Malavika Mohanan, and Krithi Shetty in attendance, it felt less like a formal showcase and more like a real moment around fashion. The room was sharp, observant, and engaged.
Fatima Sana Shaikh said it best: “Balenciaga always has a chic approach and the brand is now heading towards it even more aggressively.” And that tone carried through the evening. Nothing felt loud for attention, yet everything commanded it.
What stood out was how people responded. There was curiosity, closer inspection, and genuine interest in the details. Some silhouettes felt familiar but more refined, while others brought a quiet sense of drama without trying too hard.
That is when a collection works.
Not when it demands attention, but when it earns it.
@balenciaga #Balenciaga

posts.caption:
Jacob & Co. continues to blur the line between watchmaking, sculpture, and fantasy with the Astronomia “Gates of Olympus.”
Priced at $1.4 million, the one-of-one creation draws inspiration from Greek mythology, featuring Zeus, Olympus, celestial movement, and a fully mechanical miniature world built directly into the timepiece itself.
Inside the 18K rose gold case sits a triple-axis tourbillon, over 9 carats of baguette diamonds, and an intricate Olympus scene designed as part of the movement.
More than a watch, the piece reflects how modern haute horology is increasingly moving toward collectible art and immersive storytelling.
@JacobAndCo #JacobAndCo #Luxurious #Collab
posts.alt_text:
@Balenciaga dropped its Spring Summer 2026 preview collection in Mumbai last night, and the energy in the room felt different.

With Fatima Sana Sheikh, Bobby Deol, Mouni Roy, Sanya Malhotra, Malavika Mohanan, and Krithi Shetty in attendance, it felt less like a formal showcase and more like a real moment around fashion. The room was sharp, observant, and engaged.

Fatima Sana Shaikh said it best: “Balenciaga always has a chic approach and the brand is now heading towards it even more aggressively.” And that tone carried through the evening. Nothing felt loud for attention, yet everything commanded it.

What stood out was how people responded. There was curiosity, closer inspection, and genuine interest in the details. Some silhouettes felt familiar but more refined, while others brought a quiet sense of drama without trying too hard.

That is when a collection works.
Not when it demands attention, but when it earns it. 

@balenciaga #Balenciaga

posts.caption:
Mercedes-AMG Petronas is on track to become the first Formula 1 team in history to generate more than $1 billion in annual revenue.
The team reported revenue of over $846 million for 2025, driven by sponsorships, licensing, commercial partnerships, and on-track success. With Formula 1’s global popularity continuing to surge, Mercedes could break the billion-dollar barrier as early as next year.
The milestone highlights how Formula 1 teams have evolved from racing operations into global sports and entertainment businesses, with the world’s leading teams now rivaling major franchises across traditional sports.

posts.caption:
Formula 1 cars are among the most expensive machines in sport.
A modern F1 car is estimated to cost between $12 million and $20 million to build, with the hybrid power unit alone accounting for a significant portion of the total cost.
Every component, from the carbon-fibre chassis and aerodynamics to the advanced electronics, is engineered for maximum performance.
#Luxurious #F1

posts.caption:
#ManifestCover
There is a quiet power to Rukmini Vasanth (@rukmini_vasanth). One that doesn’t seek attention, yet holds it effortlessly.
For Manifest’s May–June cover, she steps into an imaginative world where temple jewellery meets contemporary silhouettes, blurring the lines between memory and modernity. She embodies a woman in transition, grounded, luminous, and yet constantly becoming.
“I’m not very good at describing myself,” she says, almost in passing.
Yet, in the same breath, her choices reveal a quiet clarity of intent. “I don’t want to isolate my character. I need to understand the whole world it exists in.”
Between restraint and reinvention, stillness and movement, she exists in that rare in-between. Not fully defined, not confined. Just on the cusp of a wider sky.
Our latest issue is now live on Magzter. Read the full cover story from the link in bio.
Chairperson: Avarna Jain (@avarna_jain)
CEO: Debashish Ghosh

posts.alt_text:
Priyanka Chopra Jonas (@priyankachopra) continues to show us that one can do it all, and do it on their own terms. 

For an entire generation, she’s been the blueprint for dreaming bigger. From winning Miss World in 2000 to breaking barriers in Hollywood, producing meaningful stories through Purple Pebble Pictures, championing children’s rights with UNICEF and building a truly global career, Priyanka has spent decades proving that ambition has no postcode.

And now, she’s added another jewel to her crown.

Rolex has officially welcomed Priyanka Chopra Jonas to its prestigious family of Testimonees, recognising not just her achievements, but her influence as a cultural force who continues to redefine what success looks like on a global stage.

Honestly, it feels like a fitting match. Excellence, longevity, precision and timeless impact—qualities Rolex is known for, and qualities Priyanka has embodied every step of the way. She’s definitely served up another well-timed ace, quite literally!

posts.caption:
We’ve all had that mascara stress—flakes, smudges, clumping and more. But you can now bid adieu to that. The beauty world has officially entered its effortless era, and the ‘No Mascara’ trend is taking over. Sighing in relief, already?
From Gigi Hadid to Hunter Schafer and being hot on the Spring-Summer 2026 runways, the fashion world is embracing this trend, making the case for bare lashes to be just as striking as a full-glam look. Think fresh skin, softly defined eyes and makeup that feels easy, undone and effortlessly cool.
After all, if smudged eyeliner replaced sharp wings and bare nails became the ultimate luxury statement, maybe natural lashes were always next. Plus, let’s be honest—there’s something very appealing about not spending the end of the night battling waterproof mascara.
Soft glam brides, consider this your sign. Bare lashes might just be the wedding beauty trend of 2026.
By Palak Valecha (@_palakvalecha_)
[Natural Makeup Look, Wedding Makeup Trends 2026, SS26, Minimal Makeup, Clean Girl Aesthetic, Beauty]

posts.caption:
June’s love forecast? Less chasing, more connecting.
Your Love Horoscope and stars this month are asking you to put down the dating rulebook and lean into something a little softer, something that feels right. Think meaningful conversations, unexpected crushes, spontaneous plans and the kind of chemistry that sneaks up on you when you least expect it: Hannah and Garrett from ‘Off Campus’, anyone?
Whether you’re deep in your relationship, thriving in your single era a la , navigating a situationship a la ‘Emily in Paris’, or waiting for that text to finally come through, June is all about honesty, vulnerability and being intentional with your heart.
No games. No mixed signals. Just main-character energy and a little help from the stars for your zodiac signs. ✨💫
#Manifest #ManifestingRight
[Astrology, June Horoscope, Star Signs, Relationship Advice, Horoscope 2026]

posts.caption:
Rhea Subbaiah’s (@rhea.subbaiah) wedding to Nikhil Bidapa (@nikhilbidapa) was a beautiful celebration of family, tradition and the people who shaped their story.
As Rhea puts it, “Looking back, it feels like we were always meant to find our way to each other.”
The couple had technically known each other for most of their lives, but it was Bengaluru that played cupid. Their two-day Kodava wedding brought together generations of family, meaningful rituals, heirloom jewellery, traditional music, endless dancing and the kind of warmth only a close-knit community can create.
Dressed in timeless Kanchipuram silks and heirloom Kodava jewellery passed down through generations, Rhea’s bridal looks were rooted in heritage. But what truly stood out was how personal the celebrations felt. No priests, no elaborate formalities—just the blessings of their ancestors, elders and loved ones.
Read more via the link in bio 🔗
Words: Ismat Tahseen (@ismat.tahseen)
#Manifest #Wedding
[Wedding Diary, Real Wedding]

posts.caption:
Some TV weddings are so iconic, they become part of our collective love story.
These are the wedding episodes we still revisit years later, because the best TV weddings were never just about the wedding décor, bridal fashion or dreamy venues. They were about the love stories we invested seasons in, the couples we rooted for, and the happy endings that had us laughing, crying and falling in love right alongside them.
From the slow-burn romance of Anthony Bridgerton and Kate Sharma to the chaos and charm of Monica and Chandler’s big day, the heartfelt magic of Jim and Pam’s Niagara Falls wedding, and Amy Santiago and Jake Peralta’s unforgettable Brooklyn Nine-Nine ceremony, these are the wedding episodes that set the standard for romance on screen.
Whether you’re building your wedding mood board, searching for inspiration, or simply rewatching your favourite comfort show, these are the TV weddings we keep coming back to.

posts.caption:
The most charming team outfit we’ve seen thus far at the World Cup did not derive from some sportswear juggernaut with a billion-dollar ad budget. Instead, it was the product of a self-taught 30-year-old whose brand has fewer than 10,000 Instagram followers.
Alvin Mak, a Congolese designer in Paris, is the man responsible for the leopard-embellished suits and leopard bags that the Congolese national team wore for its arrival in Houston last week. The team, which is appearing in its first World Cup since 1974, doesn’t play its first match until Wednesday, but it has already made quite an impression. Images of the team in their outfits spread broadly online, drawing comments like “when can I purchase those bags” in multiple languages.
Mak hadn’t expected such an outsize, global response and is still catching up to all the attention. “In Congo culture, the spirit of leopard is a spirit of strength,” @alvin_jmak told our reporter @jacobwgallagher. “It is the spirit of resilience, so I want to transfer this energy to them.”
At the link in our bio, learn how the Congolese team's @jmakxparis outfits came together. Photos by Troy Taormina/Imagn Images, via Reuters, Maria Lysaker/Imagn Images, via Reuters
posts.alt_text:
Last year, the designer Mike Amiri encountered the soccer effect for the first time.

F.C. Barcelona, the Spanish soccer team that has won the Champions League five times and is contributing 16 players to various World Cup teams, announced that Amiri would be its official formal wear partner for the next five years. The result of the announcement, Amiri said, trumped the effect of any of the celebrities — like Justin Bieber, Teyana Taylor and Maluma — who wear his label.

“We were a niche Los Angeles brand, doing shows in Paris, and then all of a sudden we became global,” Amiri said. Athletes like Travis Kelce, Kyle Kuzma and Odell Beckham Jr. have gravitated toward the label, not only because of the fit of the clothes, but also because Amiri took an interest in sports stars when the fashion industry ignored them.

Now, as the World Cup begins and fashion wises up to the ways that soccer can elevate brands, Amiri may be a case study in what happens next. Tap the link in our bio to read more about how @amiri is leveling up. Photos by @theirnameisricardo, Amiri, Chris Graythen/Getty Images

At the first U.S. home World Cup match against Paraguay in Los Angeles on Friday, the colors and the chants were loud with both nations’ affinities for red and white stripes on display. There was also a red, white and blue Liberty flame hat, some sombreros, and at least one pair of fans dressed in Stars and Stripes overalls.
Tap the link in our bio to see more moments of creative expression at the match. Photos by @simbarashecha

posts.caption:
David Hockney, the artist who died on Thursday at 88, had a madcap maximalism in his approach to style. As with his paintings, he favored bold colors for his clothes.
"It always seemed as if there were another Hockney outfit to discover," the fashion reporter Jacob Gallagher wrote. His style pinged around the mood boards of the fashion world for decades, showing the painter at every life stage: the young Londoner in his pink-striped rugby shirt and pulverized plimsolls, or the still whimsical Pop Art icon in mod tweed suits well into his 80s. Hockney became a north star for designers trying to get men to wear more color.
"When it comes to style, some people just have it," @jacobwgallagher wrote. "It seems to emanate from their very being. Hockney was that kind of man." Read more about Hockney's style legacy and see more photos at the link in our bio. Photos by @nathanael.turner, Anthony Barboza/Getty Images, Aaron Chown/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images, @suzanne_plunkett

posts.caption:
The details on Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce's wedding are scarce, but that doesn’t mean you can’t celebrate the event of the season. Channel your inner wedding planner and use your Swiftie knowledge to dream up your vision of the couple's nuptials.
Where will it take place? What's on the dinner menu? What about wedding favors? With our fantasy wedding planner, make your selections for what you think @taylorswift and @killatrav's big day might look like. Try it at the link in our bio, where your choices will be saved so you can share with your friends.
posts.alt_text:
The most charming team outfit we’ve seen thus far at the World Cup did not derive from some sportswear juggernaut with a billion-dollar ad budget. Instead, it was the product of a self-taught 30-year-old whose brand has fewer than 10,000 Instagram followers.

Alvin Mak, a Congolese designer in Paris, is the man responsible for the leopard-embellished suits and leopard bags that the Congolese national team wore for its arrival in Houston last week. The team, which is appearing in its first World Cup since 1974, doesn’t play its first match until Wednesday, but it has already made quite an impression. Images of the team in their outfits spread broadly online, drawing comments like “when can I purchase those bags” in multiple languages.

Mak hadn’t expected such an outsize, global response and is still catching up to all the attention. “In Congo culture, the spirit of leopard is a spirit of strength,” @alvin_jmak told our reporter @jacobwgallagher. “It is the spirit of resilience, so I want to transfer this energy to them.”

At the link in our bio, learn how the Congolese team's @jmakxparis outfits came together. Photos by Troy Taormina/Imagn Images, via Reuters, Maria Lysaker/Imagn Images, via Reuters

posts.caption:
Knicks fans don’t settle for simple T-shirts. (Not even, to the team’s dismay, free ones.)
They want to show their pride, yes, of course. But they’re not willing to squelch their sense of style entirely. This is New York after all.
On Monday night, as the Knicks had their first home finals game since Rudy Giuliani was mayor, fans have been coated in orange and blue.  Beyond that committed color scheme, though, just about anything goes.
Swipe through to see the style at Madison Square Garden and tap the link in our bio to see more photos. Photos by @simbarashecha

posts.caption:
After staging his last three seasons in Paris, #SimonePorteJacquemus will return to a jaw-dropping natural landscape, having announced the Phare de la Pietra in Corsica as the location for his next show.
Natural landscapes have become a signature of #Jacquemus shows, restoring the emotional power of the runway at a time when many have become predictable, overly commercial, or designed for digital consumption. I still remember watching the wheat-field show - it was one of those rare moments that make you fall in love with fashion all over again.
Which #Runway set was your favourite?
posts.alt_text:
First debuted for Fall 2024, the Robe Spirale was a feat of fashion engineering. At its core was a 3D-printed structure formed as one continuous coiled strip. Like a slap bracelet, it wrapped and snapped around the torso, using the natural curves of the body to hold itself securely in place.

#PieterMulier further developed the idea for Spring 2025 with a series of pleated dresses that appeared to float on the body. They gripped the torso at carefully calculated points, while hidden internal armatures carried much of the structural load. The visible fabric acted as the skin of the construction, and the lightweight pleating reduced the need for heavy internal support.

Just like #AzzedineAlaïa, @pieter_mulier obsesses with the idea of construction and rhe body merging fashion, sculpture and architecture engineering.

posts.caption:
#Balenciaga Spring 2027 may be #PierpaoloPiccioli’s clearest vision for the brand yet. Less about direct references, it focuses on #CristóbalBalenciaga’s philosophy of construction and the relationship between fabric and the body.
References to the house’s historic balloon, drape, and cocoon shapes suggest a welcome return to its heritage, but reengineered for modern life. Through new textile innovations, the collection emphasises lightness, with entire looks weighing less than a kilogram. @pppiccioli wants to demonstrate how #HauteCouture principles can be relevant to everyday life. Ordinary wardrobe staples are elevated through couture-level construction.
What do you think of the new Balenciaga so far?
posts.alt_text:

posts.caption:
One of my favourite signatures of #SarahBurton, and something I’m excited to see her continue at #Givenchy is her extraordinary couture fringe technique. First fully realised for #AlexanderMcQueen Fall 2018, she drew inspiration from insects, butterflies, and metamorphosis to create the  butterfly-wing gown, dissolving into cascading silk threads.
These garments are so fascinating because of the level of craftsmanship required. The threads are dyed, blended, layered, and hand-applied to create subtle gradients of colour and movement. I had the chance to see one IRL and the delicacy of the work is unbelievable. The fringe requires constant brushing and arrangement, with even the smallest movement causing the threads to tangle, making the garments feel almost alive.
posts.alt_text:

posts.caption:
One of my favourite, and most hauntingly beautiful details from #DanielRoseberry’s #Schiaparelli couture collections was the sheer crinoline veils/face masks, from Fall 2024. Wrapped around the face and head they functioned as sculptural forms. The material appeared almost weightless, creating distortions that blurred the contours of the face and transformed the models into embryonic works of art. Makeup by @patstingz, hair by @guidopalau
Also reminded me of #GarethPugh’s Spring 2013 collection, which featured sheer mesh and stretched fabrics wrapped around the head and face, or #MarcJacobs Spring 2019. Major inspo for brides looking for an unconventional wedding veil!
posts.alt_text:

posts.caption:
If you have any of these bags, please step forward… #KarlLagerfeld was known for his larger-than-life shows, and the spectacle didn’t just stop at the sets, but translated into his absurd yet highly collectible bags.
Think of the iconic hula hoop bag from Spring 2013, which Karl joked was designed for the beach so you could stick it into the sand and hang your towel from it (which fyi someone is currently selling on eBay for £145K.) Then there was the supermarket show, complete with milk carton bags and the #Chanel chain basket, or the Spring 2019 flap bag filled with sand.
The bags became souvenirs from those fantasy worlds and now #MatthieuBlazy is continuing that tradition, turning handbags into collectible objects that blur the line between fashion and art.
Which one is your favourite?

posts.caption:
Justin Bieber for Issue 01: “The First Ever” Outlander Magazine In-Print!
This is a real piece of history for us all. The past 10 years of lessons and learning have led up to this moment and I couldn’t be happier to share it with you all.
Some of you may be new followers who discovered us last week and some of you may have been here since day 1, either way this is for everyone who aligns with what it means to be an outlander.
Also, this is a great time to introduce myself for the first time in 10 years. My name is @callum.mccaff & we are all Outlander. I was born in 1996 so we priced the magazine at £19.96.
Available worldwide at www.outlandermag.com
PRINT ISN’T DEAD.
🫂 @lilbieber
📸 @sweetcian
🔗 @freenjoy
posts.alt_text:
Naomi Osaka for Outlander Magazine wearing her custom Nike Roland Garros looks designed by Nike & Kevin Germanier!

Roland Garros’ best dressed just raised the bar again. Naomi’s custom Nike looks were designed by Kevin Germanier, the couture designer turning upcycled materials into something iconic. Clay court season needed a moment like this, so we took her to an authentic orange clay court in paris to match the energy she’ll be surrounded by this week😤🤝

posts.caption:
Travis Scott for Issue 01: “The First Ever” Outlander Magazine In-Print!
GANG, TRAVIS SCOTT IN HERMÈS FOR OUR FIRST EVER MAGAZINE COVER. THIS IS UNHEARD OF, AND BETTER YET? IT WAS FOR OUTLANDER MAGAZINE. I AM HUMBLED.
WOW, what a moment. My only wish in this life is that what I’ve spent the last 10 years building acts as a strong form of inspiration for your own creative careers - I came from no fashion background, I had no connections & I had no idea what I was doing, but, I did it anyway.
If you didn’t know by now, my name is @callum.mccaff , I founded Outlander - and I’m here to remind everyone that PRINT ISN’T DEAD.
Available worldwide at www.outlandermag.com

posts.caption:
What you’re looking at is the LEVIS stadium but…
Levis had to cover its own branding because of FIFA’s strict “clean stadium” rules, which forbid the display of any corporate names or logos that are not official World Cup sponsors. Because Levi’s is not a sponsor, the stadium’s logo was covered up
So Levi changed their IG photo to match the logo-free advertising😤

posts.caption:
That’s right, the village IS the hotel
It’s called Sextantio which is an innovative hospitality and cultural preservation project in Italy that transformed the abandoned historic hamlets into scattered hotels. So, Instead of a single building, the hotel rooms, reception, and restaurants are dispersed throughout the actual medieval villages, allowing guests to stay in authentic, restored historic structures alongside local residents🤯

posts.caption:
India's Renee Noronha has won first place in the 18-24 category at the Ironman Philippines. Completing the gruelling challenge at 15:55:37, the 20-year-old IIT Madras student upheld held her record of being the youngest Indian female Ironman finisher.
Renee battled torrential rains and unforgiving temperatures to complete the race. In conversation with SheThePeople, the triathlete also revealed that she participated on day 3 of her period, making the victory a profound reminder of her strength and resilience as a woman.
With this win, Renee has now qualified for the Ironman World Championship Hawaii, scheduled for October 2026. She is also on a mission to set a new Guinness world record to become the youngest person in the world to do a full Ironman on 6 continents. She is on track to achieve this by April 2027, at the age of 21.
Read full interview at www.shethepeople.tv. | @renee.noronha
#Ironman #Philippines #sports full Ironman challenge, women in sports, women in endurance sports, mumbai sports, Chennai sports, IIT Madras student, women in STEM, ironman Kona, triathlon, marathon, menstrual awareness
posts.alt_text:
FIFA has officially selected and accredited Assamese photojournalist Gitika Talukdar to cover the upcoming 2026 FIFA World Cup. This will be her third time covering the Men's FIFA World Cup.

Prior to this, Gitika has covered the 2018 Russia World Cup, the 2022 Qatar FIFA World Cup, the 2019 France Women's FIFA World Cup, the 2023 Women's FIFA World Cup in Australia and New Zealand, the 2020 Tokyo Olympic Games, the Paris Olympic Games, and others.

Visit www.shethepeople.tv for more

#FIFA #worldcup #photography sports photography sports photojournalist sports journalist indian media india at FIFA World Cup 2026 north america football news soccer news

posts.caption:
Anjali Sivaraman - Actor, Model, Musician & Cat Mom - chooses characters that go against the grain of what women are "supposed" to be like. "Society often tries to put women into a mould but we are so layered," said @anjalisivaraman, speaking to SheThePeople about female representation in cinema.
Anjali has consistently chosen roles that are bold, rebellious, and often rough around the edges. The fauji kid-turned-artiste admits that she brings a tinge of her real-life badass personality into her characters.
Anjali is best known for her roles in 'Bad Girl', directed by Varsha Bharath, and the Netflix suspense-drama series 'Class'. She is set to star in Amrita Sher-Gil's biopic 'Amri' by Mira Nair.
Full interview by Tanya Savkoor coming soon on www.shethepeople.tv | Stay tuned!
#anjalisivaraman #bollywood #webseries bollywood movies Hindi web series Hindi movies Tamil movies Tamil films Tamil cinema Hindi cinema feminist films feminist movies feminist stories

posts.caption:
The World Cup soon ‘kicks’ off. And soon, streets and sofas in front of televisions or people planted on chairs in restaurants will be adorned with unique jerseys and even more unique celebration methods.
Today, it’s hard to spot a single fan who’s not wearing a jersey while a football game is on. However, it no longer remains confined to the limits of a stadium. It’s a statement. Oftentimes, people are more than happy to wear dupes of a team’s jersey they couldn’t recognise with a magnifying glass. Nevertheless, an authentic jersey is equivalent to a prized heirloom.
However, when was it that this jersey-mania became prevalent? Like every trend, there isn’t a singular answer but a journey to take you through.
Opinion: @the_haute_historian | Visit www.shethepeople.tv to read more
#fifaworldcup2026 #football #jersey #fashion
Football jerseys, fashion, Football players, Dua lipa, Bella Hadid, stadiums

posts.caption:
In a recent example of women making a landmark move, IPS officer Ananya Awasthi has been appointed as the Aide-de-Camp (ADC) to the Governor of Odisha. Awasthi, a 2022-batch IPS officer of the Odisha cadre, previously served as Assistant Commissioner of Police (ACP), UPD, Cuttack.
A formal induction ceremony was held at Lok Bhavan, where Governor Dr Hari Babu Kambhampati welcomed Awasthi to her new role as the Police ADC.
Ananya Awasthi’s appointment indicates the growing presence of women in leadership. She has become an inspiration for many young girls, as she became the first woman officer to serve as ADC to the Governor of Odisha.
Visit www.shethepeople.tv to read more
#ananyaawasthi #adc #odisha #ips
Ananya Awasthi, IPS officer, inspiration, Odisha, Aide-de-Camp, Hari Babu Kambhampati, Raj bhavan of Odisha, Indian security services, women in leadership, women in police, women empowerment, encouragement
posts.alt_text:
India's Renee Noronha has won first place in the 18-24 category at the Ironman Philippines. Completing the gruelling challenge at 15:55:37, the 20-year-old IIT Madras student upheld held her record of being the youngest Indian female Ironman finisher.

Renee battled torrential rains and unforgiving temperatures to complete the race. In conversation with SheThePeople, the triathlete also revealed that she participated on day 3 of her period, making the victory a profound reminder of her strength and resilience as a woman.

With this win, Renee has now qualified for the Ironman World Championship Hawaii, scheduled for October 2026. She is also on a mission to set a new Guinness world record to become the youngest person in the world to do a full Ironman on 6 continents. She is on track to achieve this by April 2027, at the age of 21.

Read full interview at www.shethepeople.tv. | @renee.noronha

#Ironman #Philippines #sports full Ironman challenge, women in sports, women in endurance sports, mumbai sports, Chennai sports, IIT Madras student, women in STEM, ironman Kona, triathlon, marathon, menstrual awareness

posts.caption:
A beach, a red lehenga, and the kind of quiet that only happens when two people have nothing left to prove to each other.
No grand backdrop needed. No elaborate setting. Just open sand stretching in every direction, a bride wearing her excitement in the softest smile, and a groom standing beside her with the kind of certainty that doesn’t announce itself - it just is. Still, steady, and entirely sure.
That is what love looks like when it has found its person.
And then there was them. ❤️
But what made Kaajal and Vishal’s portraits linger long after the last frame was how effortlessly they held every kind of moment - the laughter that broke through without warning, the quiet glances that said everything the words didn’t need to, and two people moving through their portraits like they had forgotten the camera was there entirely.
Some couple portraits are beautiful.
And some stay with you because they felt true - the beach unhurried, the red vivid against the pale sand, and a love story that needed no perfect moment because it had found the perfect person. 🌟
Couple: @vishal0310 & @kaajal.s
Photos Courtesy: @yourpastelmoments
#JustChekhmateIt

posts.caption:
Fashion styling is a job. Not a hobby. Not a passion project. Not something people do on the side because they love clothes.
It is a profession that takes years to build. The eye. The contacts. The knowledge of how the industry actually works across every sector, every brief, every type of client. That does not come from loving fashion. It comes from years of showing up, doing the work and figuring it out mostly alone.
And yet it is still one of the most undervalued roles in the creative industry.
Stylists are asked to work for free. For credit. For exposure. For a rate that does not come close to reflecting the skill, the hours or the experience involved. And somewhere along the way the industry decided that was normal. That stylists should be grateful for the opportunity. That loving what you do means you should not expect to be paid properly for it.
We are past that.
Behind every iconic image, every red carpet moment, every campaign that stopped you mid-scroll, there was a stylist. Building the looks, interpreting the brief, managing the pressure, absorbing the chaos so nobody else had to. Often without a credit. Sometimes without a proper rate.
That changes when stylists know their worth, charge it without apology and stop accepting the jobs that treat their career like a favour.
➡️➡️ If you are a working fashion stylist who is ready to do exactly that, comment STUDIO and we will send you the free trial link to The Studio of Stylist Elixir. Rate guides. Contracts. Portfolio tools. Masterclasses every Monday. Everything you need to build a career that reflects what you are actually worth.
Save this. Send it to a stylist who needs to hear it.

Jonathan Anderson just showed his first couture collection for Dior. If you do not understand why it mattered, this post is for you.

John Galliano was front row. The artisans were the opening act. The silhouettes came from ceramicist Magdalene Odundo.

If you are in Paris: the show space at Musée Rodin opens to the public from 28 January for one week. The new couture collection, archival Dior pieces, and the Odundo ceramics that inspired it. You can see it yourself.

This is not about liking or not liking a collection. This is about understanding what you are looking at.
The Studio of Stylist Elixir is our online membership. Guides. Templates. Live calls. A community of stylists who are learning and building together. Comment ‘studio’ for a free trial.

posts.caption:
The things a fashion stylist carries that no one ever sees.
The quiet fear you’ve plateaued. Watching work you wanted go to someone else, and being
happy for them while it still stings. The guilt of wanting more when you already have jobs others
would kill for. Never feeling quite ready to reach for the dream clients you can clearly see.
Here’s what no one tells you. None of that means you’ve peaked. It means you’ve outgrown
waiting to be chosen. Your dream clients were never going to find you, you go to them. And
naming the work you want isn’t ungrateful. It’s the first real move toward booking it.
Save this if you’ve carried any of it. And send it to a stylist who has too.
Are you booking your dream clients?

You’re waiting to feel ready. A better portfolio, a bit more experience, a few more followers, then you’ll reach out. But that day never quite comes, does it.
Here’s the thing no one says: your dream client isn’t out there looking for you. Not because you’re not good enough. They’re just busy running their own world.
So you have to go first. Every stylist booking the work you want stopped waiting a while ago.
They didn’t get discovered. They reached out. It started with a list of ten names, and one real message to one real person.
That’s the whole thing you’ve been afraid of.
Save this for the next time you catch yourself waiting. And send it to a fashion stylist who needs
the push.

posts.caption:
Celebrity make-up artists in India have been shaping faces, trends and bridal moodboards for years. The beauty boom is here. The artist-led empire is still missing.
Globally, names like Charlotte Tilbury, Pat McGrath and Bobbi Brown turned artistry into authority, and authority into billion-dollar beauty businesses. In India, the same conversion has been slower. Local artists have built trust across skin tones, textures, climates and undertones, often doing the kind of real-world R&D global brands ignored for years.
They know what oxidises on brown skin. They know what collapses in Mumbai humidity. They know what survives a wedding, a shoot and a close-up.
Yet many remain tied to the wedding economy, where bridal bookings offer faster, steadier returns than the long, expensive work of building a brand. The result is a strange beauty paradox: India has the talent, the market and the demand. What it still lacks is the machinery.
Read more at the link in bio.
By Ria Bhatia (@riabbhatia)
#makeupbrandsinindia #indianmakeupbrands #beautyindustry #celebritymakeupartists #colourcosmetics
posts.alt_text:
Celebrating the rise of the Godawan began with a simple question: how does a bird return to public memory?

A bird that builds its nest directly on the ground is especially vulnerable when people keep taking over its habitat. Once considered a candidate for India’s national bird, the Great Indian Bustard, locally known as Godawan, has gradually disappeared from public consciousness over the decades due to habitat loss and human encroachment.

Today, its conservation story is gaining renewed attention. In Rajasthan, breeding and hatchery programmes have brought cautious progress, with new hatchlings emerging in protected facilities. Conservation efforts combine habitat restoration, protected nesting areas, water management, predator control, captive breeding, and community-led monitoring of breeding grounds.

On World Environment Day, one of India’s most iconic monuments became a canvas for this conservation story, as Qutb Minar hosted projection mapping that highlighted the Godawan’s ecological role in India’s grassland ecosystems and the challenges facing its survival, with support from Godawan Estuary Premium Water.

posts.caption:
Solo dining in India still comes with unsolicited concern. People assume you’ve been stood up, abandoned by friends, or are being brave in a way nobody asked for.
But younger Indians are changing the script. Gen Z and millennials are showing up alone at restaurants, runs, quizzes, food walks, gigs and climbing walls. At Bangalore Climbing Initiatives, nearly 80 per cent of people come solo. Restaurants are adding counter seating and smaller formats so a table for one feels less like a logistical problem.
The solo hang is no longer just about being alone. It is about choosing your time, your interests and the people you might meet along the way.
Read more at the link in bio.
By Karishma Kuenzang ( @kkuenzang)
#solodining #solodates #urbanloneliness #genzindia #thirdspaces

Yet many remain tied to the wedding economy, where bridal bookings offer faster, steadier returns than the long, expensive work of building a brand. The result is a strange beauty paradox: India has the talent, the market and the demand. What it still lacks is the machinery.

Read more at the link in bio.
By Ria Bhatia (@riabbhatia) 

#makeupbrandsinindia #indianmakeupbrands #beautyindustry #celebritymakeupartists #colourcosmetics

posts.caption:
Depression is often described through what it takes away: energy, sleep, appetite, motivation and a sense of self. Far less often is it discussed through the lens of intimacy.
A reduced sex drive is one of the most common experiences associated with depression, yet it remains largely absent from conversations around mental health. For many people, the loss of desire arrives alongside emotional exhaustion, physical fatigue and the daily effort of simply getting through the day. The challenges can continue even after treatment begins, with antidepressants carrying sexual side effects that many patients are unaware of until they experience them.
The impact extends beyond the individual, shaping relationships, communication and self-perception in ways that are rarely acknowledged. As therapist Aanandita Vagahani puts it: “It’s often one of the first things to go and one of the last things to come back—and that has enormous consequences for relationships and self-worth.”
Read more about how depression and sex reshape intimacy by Ria Bhatia (@riabbhatia) at the link in bio
#Depression #MentalHealth #SexualHealth #IntimacyMatters #TheEstablished

posts.caption:
Skincare has moved from bottles to jars and from serums to supplements. Peptide supplements are now headlining the edible skincare era, promising beauty from within.
But dermatologists caution: while oral collagen peptides show modest improvements in hydration and wrinkles, the claims around hair growth and miracle fixes are overstated.
The bigger story? Supplements are becoming lifestyle products, consumed by 70% of Indians without doctor consultation, driven by marketing that often blurs science.
The future of beauty may be edible, but it’s also a reminder to question the hype.
Read more at the link in bio.
By Ria Bhatia (@riabbhatia)
#Peptides #Supplements #SkinHealth #CollagenPeptides #BeautyCulture

posts.caption:
Rule of thumb: never skip a Paul Smith campaign. Walton Goggins recently returned as the face of Paul Smith for AW26, this time getting a crash course in sartorial etiquette from Sir Paul himself.
The campaign unfolds across four short films directed by Cameron Turnbull, with stills by Samuel Bradley, set against the British countryside. Styled by Ben Schofield, it leans into everything @paulsmithdesign does best: heritage fabrics like Harris Tweed and Donegal wool, offset with just enough wit to keep it from feeling too proper.
Tie rules, the sock dilemma in shoes-off households, the “Sometimes, Always, Never” jacket principle—it’s all there, ending with Sir Paul stepping in to inspect the final look.
#PaulSmith #WaltonGoggins #FashionCampaign #MenswearStyle #Tailoring
posts.alt_text:
Bring me here ASAP. Built between 1902 and 1930, Lluís Domènech i Montaner designed Hospital de la Santa Creu i Sant Pau around a radical idea for its time: that sunlight, gardens, color, and beautiful architecture could actively help heal the human body.

While a modern hospital still operates next door, the original Art Nouveau pavilions have since become a UNESCO World Heritage Site: a sprawling “garden city” of tiled facades, mosaic details, palm-lined pathways, and stained glass flooded with natural light.

A genuinely therapeutic place to spend an afternoon.

#Barcelona #ArtNouveau #Architecture #Travel #TheCitizensPoste

This skincare brand was inspired by the desert, and I’m so into it. Founded by three lifelong friends from Palm Springs, @760skin was built on the idea that skincare should be simple, effective, and able to hold up under pressure (much like the environment that inspired it).
So we spoke with founders Mia Heckmann, Madison Heckmann, and Olivia Halton about building with intention in a space that moves fast and forgets even faster. And before you ask: yes, I’ve tried it. It works.

posts.caption:
Fatherhood is a new role for the actor who first got noticed for his performance in ‘Masaan’ (2015). In fact, it seems this off-camera Vicky Kaushal (@vickykaushal09) is perhaps closer to Deepak Chaudhary, the character he played in the film. A sensitive, ambitious young man coming to terms with the world around him. That character, Kaushal says, changed him. But not for the obvious reasons.
“Until then success, to me, was where you reached [in life]. It was never about where you started from,” he explains. “That character, starting from the crematorium ghat in Benaras and going on to work as an engineer in the Railways in Allahabad [now Prayagraj]... He had a far bigger life arc than I’ve covered in my life. Till today,” he says, tapping the arm rest for emphasis. “After that I started having so much empathy for those around me, especially those from far less privileged backgrounds, because I don’t know where they have started from.”
@travellerprasad in conversation with the Bollywood actor at the link in bio.

posts.caption:
Thirty minutes after this interview was supposed to begin, Vicky Kaushal’s (@vickykaushal09) tall, statuesque frame bursts in through the door at the Matrix office in Khar, Mumbai. “So sorry, bro,” he says, shaking this reporter’s hand with both of his. His day has been busy—his son’s visa was to be sorted, he couldn’t leave his wife alone, and there’s repair work going on in his building, he says, sounding like your average young husband and father with his daily household chores to take care of, not some Bollywood star with a platoon of staff to handle his every whim.
Since becoming a father, Kaushal says he’s come to understand and appreciate “a lot more” of the work his parents did in raising him. “It occurs to me at the most random moments that that [instilling the right values] is now my responsibility towards my son,” he says. “He is going to learn by observing how his parents are thinking, talking, and behaving. So, I’ve realised whatever you want to see reflected in him has to come in your actions rather than words.”
@travellerprasad in conversation with the Bollywood actor at the link in bio.

posts.caption:
Meghalaya is shown as an unmissable backdrop in Reble’s (@rebleofficial) music video ‘Praying Mantis’, where stunts, friends, and familiar hangouts all make an appearance as the rapper gives us fiery bars about how she’s not one to be messed with.
The artist has scored Bollywood bangers like ‘Aari Aari’ and ‘Run Down the City – Monica’ from the Dhurandhar movies and raged to nu-metal territories on ‘New Riot’ in the past year. Fresh off a performance at BUDX NBA House 2026 in New Delhi this month, where she performed the brooding ‘Praying Mantis’, Reble owns her identity as a rapper from the northeast while telling us there are more layers.
Beyond living root bridges, misty hills, and the tag of India’s rock capital, the Shillong that Reble knows is about hanging out in studios as a teenager, performing across the northeast and recharging in markets and food spots.
At the link in bio, @anuragtagat chats with the upcoming artist about what she would show a first-timer in Meghalaya.

posts.caption:
There is currently an entire corner of the internet dedicated to cultivating better taste. And because everyone already has access to everything now, what people are really looking for is curation: somebody with enough taste, specificity, and point of view to tell them what is actually worth paying attention to. Few places capture that shift better than Shreeji News & Magazines (@shreejinews).
The newsagent itself has stood on London’s Chiltern Street since the 1950s, but its current chapter began in 1982 when the current owner, Sandeep Garg, took over the business. Over the last few years, it has evolved into one of those rare places that people in fashion, beauty, publishing, and design keep returning to because it feels thoughtfully edited rather than endlessly stocked. Somebody has clearly considered why something deserves to be there before putting it on a shelf.
At the link in bio, @sheyakurian on the London shop to know for anyone looking to cultivate better taste.

posts.caption:
A few weeks ago, when I walked into the new 11:11 / eleven eleven store in Delhi’s Lodhi Market, my usually taut focus zone began to elasticate rapidly. No one product or person angles for dominance here, despite the multiplicity of elements. A sturdy yet malleable bamboo arch peacefully burdened with ready-to-wear, indigo dyeing vats, ceramic miniatures, ruralism through architecture and fashion contemporaneity populate the space. In merchandise categories you have hand-painted sneakers, bandhani tunics, dresses and shirts—the brand’s signature pieces—appliquéd jackets, Kala Cotton denims, pyjamas and bed covers. Designer Shani Himanshu’s appliqué pieces look three-dimensional, but they are the godchildren of the multidimensional texture of his space. House colour Indigo runs through the store—as hue, intent and process.
One of my earlier articles on Himanshu’s work, It Takes a Village to Make a Dress, took off its boots to come and sit with me in the store. Clay, Kala Cotton and craftsmanship become the vocabulary of modernity. It is expressed through architecture, engineering and precision in the space, part of a 80-year-old structure.
The word “Blueprint” in the headline here is ironic because blue is an entire philosophy here. This is a maker’s studio masquerading as a fashion store, a republic where process outranks product. The products are not conclusions; they are outcomes. Painstaking, creative making is the main event.
The massy chronology of fashion, where product rules—has been rearranged. Artisanal skill, dyeing, bamboo and brick come before merchandising. The store assumes that knowledge—of the designer and artisan—and aesthetics possess intrinsic cultural value and must be exhibited.
From the retail area, you cross an indigo-vat passage to a bespoke fittings corner, where a glass ceiling pulls natural light in to play “fashion-fashion”. This is a deceptively simple space. Instead of modernising craft, Himanshu lets contemporaneity emerge from craft itself. Most stores sell objects. This one tells you how objects come into being. Some may simply come for the clothes and that would be perfectly legitimate.
🖋️ @shefatwork

posts.caption:
Dismantling the mythology of Rafael Nadal as an indestructible force of nature, the new Netflix documentary, Rafa, humanises the legend. It traces the persistent pain from a foot condition, other injuries and anxiety that accompanied one of tennis’ most extraordinary careers. At a time when contemporary culture is questioning narratives that glorify suffering as the price of success, the documentary leaves viewers with the question: is it worth it? Link in bio.

posts.caption:
Hi, I’m Veronica 👋🏼 a sewing pattern designer based in Melbourne, Australia.
I create patterns, tools and resources that help you sew with understanding.
My work focuses on how fabrics behave, how garments are constructed, and how sewing connects us to the history of what we wear.
Sewing Patterns
My patterns are designed for versatility, wearability and fun. They include the Hazel, Freya, Odette, Audrey, Hera, Cordelia and the Sleeve Studio — each combining comfort with thoughtful design details. You’ll also find The Noelle, a free beginner-friendly pattern that introduces my approach to fit and construction.
The Paper Doll
A print-at-home paper doll designed to help you visualise and play with my sewing patterns. You can cut it out and colour it yourself, or use the pre-filled versions featuring my fabric prints and colour palettes. The set includes curvier model options and three skin tones to choose from.
The Conscious Consumer eBook
A practical guide to fabrics, sustainability and sewing with intention. It helps you understand fibre origins, weave structures and how to make long-lasting choices when you sew or buy clothes.
Digital Sewing Tools
Two paid calculators designed for precision:
• Shirring Calculator – works out fabric shrinkage and elastic thread length.
• GSM Calculator – estimates fabric weight and drape.
Available individually or as a discounted bundle.
My PDF sewing patterns are stocked digitally through @fabricgodmother , @spotlightstores , @thefoldline , @joans.finefabric and @thedrapersdaughter .
Fabric and Accessories
You can shop my fabric designs on @spoonflower and find my illustrated phone cases at @thedairy .
Everything I create is designed to help you sew with confidence, understanding and creativity.
For all enquires email us at hello@veronicatuckerthelabel.com
posts.alt_text:
In Homer’s The Iliad, Andromache was weaving her husband a protective cloth while he was dying outside the walls of Troy.

She didn’t know yet, she just kept weaving for him to protect him. 

Women have been doing this for thousands of years, weaving protection into cloth, stitching it at the openings of garments, laying it beside sick children, tying it around the feet of people destined to find each other.

The full essay is on Substack now 🔗 link in bio.

posts.caption: About textile crafts - magical power - women: When you have a look at the old fairytales of Germany, France and Northern Europe, these stories are also filled with that pattern. In the "Nibelungenlied" (the story of Sigfried the Dragonslayer, who gets to bath in dragonsblood which makes him uninjurable besides a very small spot on his shoulder and later gets killed through that spot by Hagen von Tronje) Krimhild, Sigfrieds Wife wants to protect him and markes the spot on his tunic with red thread. Also in the beginning of the story she and her women (she is a princess, so this is her household staff) are described to make lots of clothes for the men, also to embroider them. They did it for protection, also for presenting their social status.

posts.caption:
Every culture created a goddess who presided over making, but not all of them did it the same way.
This edition introduced two categories.
The first: goddesses whose mythology is explicitly about weaving (documented, primary sources, this is what the texts actually say).
The second: goddesses that weavers chose: figures of creativity, wisdom and skill that women at the loom reached toward deliberately, even though weaving wasn’t the centre of their story.
Both categories are true, just in different ways.
And here is what I keep coming back to across all three editions of the First Makers series. You never find a GOD of weaving. Not once, across any culture. Every time, the act of making cloth belongs to the feminine divine.
That is the oldest memory we have of what women’s knowledge meant.
Full episode on The Culture of Cloth podcast 🎧 link in bio. Start there if you’re new here.
Save this and share it with someone who makes things.
Cover art: Court Ladies Preparing Newly Woven Silk, a Chinese silk painting by Emperor Huizong of Song, early 12th century.

posts.caption:
Six months of research, four in-depth online sessions and one of the oldest stories ever told 📕
I’m joining @readingsbooks Big Book Club for a deep dive into Homer’s Odyssey. It’s an online session running every Thursday from 18 June through to the Christopher Nolan film screening on 16 July.
Penelope. Athena. Circe. Helen. Nausicaa. These women have been living in my research for months and this series is the moment I get to go deeper with scholars who’ve spent their careers inside this text.
I’ll be sharing Substack reflections after each session and the first one is free, with sessions 2–5 are for paid subscribers.
Link in bio for the Readings online membership.
In partnership with @readingsbooks

posts.caption:

posts.caption: Eyes, lips, face, and… strands? @elfcosmetics is launching #haircare, marking the brand’s first category expansion after its official skincare rollout in June 2022. “This is a new category for us,” Elf Brands president Kory Marchisotto told US editor @maddieschulz. “We wanted to make sure that the formulas were perfected.” Read the full interview, including details of the product range, at the link in bio.

I’m especially excited to see the expansion into NYC. Affordable, preventative healthcare feels increasingly out of reach for so many people, and innovations like this could help move the industry in a much needed direction.

Tomorrow, men’s trade show @pittiuomo_official opens its gates, marking the start of the Spring/Summer 2027 menswear season. So what can we expect from the shows and presentations ahead? posts.caption: @IsaacMizrahiNY has been named @Target’s creative director at large, a newly created role for the retailer that’s a homecoming of sorts for the designer. “You don’t have to spend $8,000 on a sweater for it to be amazing. A good sweater can be $45,” says Mizrahi. “And I feel like Target invented that — we did that. So it’s fertile ground now for me to continue to prove that. It’s a bigger world now, but I feel like now it’s time for Target to claim that original stake.” @HilaryMilnes shares more details at the link in bio.

posts.caption:
Bottega Veneta is betting on mycelium. Earlier this month, the Kering-owned brand released a limited collection of small leather goods swapping the bovine leather in its signature intrecciato weave for Ephea, a mycelium-based alternative by Italian biomaterials startup Sqim. The pieces are part of the fall 2026 men’s collection by creative director Louise Trotter.
Bottega Veneta’s mycelium collection is a step in the right direction, says Kering chief sustainability and institutional affairs officer Marie-Claire Daveu. @bellawebb_ shares more details at the link in bio.

Read the full cover story at the link in bio.

posts.caption:
Chaos is something @GracieAbrams has grown accustomed to. Eight years ago, she was a first-year student at Barnard with a cult fandom she’d fostered through Instagram; soon she was signed to Interscope, then on her first tour, then opening for Olivia Rodrigo and Taylor Swift. By the end of 2024, Abrams was a Grammy-nominated pop star filling arenas.
She finally took a breather last summer. “It’s not until you stop that you realize ‘Oh, I haven’t seen my friends in a long time. I haven’t cooked a meal in a long time,’” Abrams says. “The sleep schedule becomes regular for the first time in, like, four years.”
Now 26, she’s shifting into a new gear. Abrams’s third studio album, “Daughter from Hell,” out in July, sounds like her—earnest, introspective, folk-informed—but the production is more lush (“It feels like me in progress,” she says); she’s also in a happy relationship and making her first film, “Please,” with the filmmaker Halina Reijn.
For Vogue’s Summer 2026 issue, Brittany Spanos meets Gracie Abrams in Los Angeles and New York to talk about growing up, slowing down, falling in love, and where she’d like to go next. Tap the link in our bio to read the full profile.
Photographed by @larissahofmann, Written by @brittanyspanos, Styled by @jordenbickham, Hair by @tamastuzes, Makeup by @emikaneko, Manicure by @1.800.nailme, Tailoring by Irina Tshartaryan for Susie’s Custom Designs, Produced by @hyperion.la, Set Design by  @patienceharding. Shot on location @lucasmuseum. Vogue, Summer 2026.

posts.caption:
As former model Chris Royer explained to Vogue, the best way to understand what it was like to work with Duane Michals is to use the Hungarian term kincsvadászat, which essentially means a “treasure hunt.” For Royer, a Duane Michals story was exactly that: “You were going on a trip, you knew you were going to discover things, it was a creative treasure hunt.” Michals who passed away on June 9th,  contributed regularly to Vogue during the 1970s and ’80s. His work during that period ranged from shooting stills of Robert Redford and Mia Farrow in costume on set while filming The Great Gatsby; to reportage of the San Francisco ballet and jewelry designer Elsa Peretti hard at work in her legendary studio-apartment; to portraits of notable men, including Yves Saint Laurent, Dudley Moore, and Philip Glass. Take a look back at his legacy at the link in bio.
1) Photographed by Duane Michals, Vogue, October 1976
2) Photographed by Duane Michals, Vogue, February 1976
3) Photographed by Duane Michals, Vogue, April 15, 1972
4) Photographed by Duane Michals, Vogue, October 1976
5) Photographed by Duane Michals, Vogue, October 1976
posts.alt_text: While watching the 2026 NBA Finals at home, you may have noticed a dapper older gentleman in an outrageous suit among the stars. That would be @NYKnicks Legend Walt “Clyde” Frazier, who led the team to two championships in the 1970s. His singular approach to dressing has been carefully chronicled and graded (on a letter scale) by the site “ClydesoFly.com,” going back to 2013. Naturally, he doesn’t buy off the rack. Frazier’s suits were made by tailors in New York, mostly from fabric he plucked from shops in the Garment District and Lower East Side. Learn more at his dapper ensembles at the link in bio.

posts.caption: Major change is afoot in Sweden, a fact highlighted by the two shows that bookended Stockholm Fashion Week, by provocative and kinky Gullbo on one side and the ruminatively minimalist Leoní on the other. Not only did these collections represent two vastly different ends of an aesthetic spectrum, but they also marked a generational shift (which was also evident in street style). At the link in bio, find a lowdown of the interesting, youthful, melange that was STHLMFW 2026.
